title: rumex simpliciflorus subsp. maderensis
intro: Rumex simpliciflorus subsp. maderensis, also known as Maderense Rumex, is a subspecies of the Rumex simpliciflorus plant, a type of sorrel that belongs to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of oxalic acid, which has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and inflammation. The health benefits of Rumex simpliciflorus subsp. maderensis include its ability to act as a natural diuretic, reducing water retention and alleviating symptoms of urinary tract infections. Rumex simpliciflorus subsp. maderensis can be prepared as an herbal tea by infusing its leaves in hot water, or used as a tincture in traditional medicine.
